exampleSentencesTranslated: I did not doubt that this would happen to me.
She doubted that he had forgotten the appointment date, so she called to remind him again.
I doubted she was angry, so I texted to apologize right away.

example.en: I didn't expect that you would come.
example.vi: Tôi không ngờ rằng bạn lại đến.

definitions.vi: nghi ngờ hoặc nghi vấn điều gì đó

notes:
ngờ = to doubt or suspect something might be true (but you’re not sure).

It’s a verb. You usually use it before a whole idea or situation, not just a single noun. Very often used in the negative form không ngờ = “unexpectedly / didn’t expect at all.”

Close to nghi ngờ (stronger, more explicit “to suspect”). Opposite feeling: tin (to believe).
exampleSentences: Anh không ngờ chuyện này lại xảy ra với mình.
Chị ngờ anh quên ngày hẹn nên gọi nhắc lại.
Em ngờ cô ấy giận nên nhắn tin xin lỗi ngay.
notes_md:
**ngờ** = to doubt or suspect something might be true (but you’re not sure).

It’s a verb. You usually use it before a whole idea or situation, not just a single noun. Very often used in the negative form **không ngờ** = “unexpectedly / didn’t expect at all.”


Close to **nghi ngờ** (stronger, more explicit “to suspect”). Opposite feeling: **tin** (to believe).
